questions: Does the CLI correctly send X-Helix-Org-ID header only for hxi_ key requests with orgId set?
Does hlx org switch for hxi_ keys correctly preserve the API key and update only local config?
Is the JWT org switch behavior completely unchanged?
Does hlx org list correctly mark the current org using local config for hxi_ keys?

answers.answer: Yes. The header is set at http.ts L59-61 only when both config.orgId is truthy AND config.apiKey.startsWith('hxi_'). JWT requests never include the header regardless of config.orgId.

Yes. The hxi_ branch at switch.ts L50-53 calls saveConfig({ orgId, orgName }) which does a read-merge-write preserving the existing apiKey. It never calls /api/auth/switch-org and never replaces config.apiKey.
evidence: src/org/switch.ts L50-53: saveConfig with orgId/orgName only
src/lib/config.ts L62-73: saveConfig read-merge-write preserves existing fields

answers.answer: Yes. The JWT else branch at switch.ts L54-71 retains the exact existing behavior: calls /api/auth/switch-org, saves the new accessToken as apiKey, and saves orgId/orgName from the server response.

Yes. The marker at list.ts L20-22 uses a ternary: for hxi_ keys it compares org.id === config.orgId (local selection); for JWT tokens it compares org.id === data.organization.id (server state, unchanged).
evidence: src/org/list.ts L20-22: config-based marker for hxi_ keys

statement: CLI-side code review complete. No issues found requiring fixes. All three changed files (http.ts, switch.ts, list.ts) correctly implement the ticket requirements: X-Helix-Org-ID header emission for hxi_ keys, local-only org switch preserving the API key, and config-based org list marker. JWT token behavior is completely unchanged in all files. Two minor UX observations noted (CUID orgName, no CUID pre-validation) but neither affects correctness.
